name: databases
context: Create, query, and manage Notion databases with filters and sorts
info: This skill covers creating Notion databases, querying them with compound filters and multi-key sorts, managing database property schemas, and paginating through large result sets. Notion databases are collections of pages where each page has typed properties defined by the database schema. The query endpoint supports rich filtering (equals, contains, starts_with, before, after, and/or/not compound filters) and multi-column sorting. Results are cursor-paginated with configurable page sizes up to 100. Use @notionhq/client to interact with the Databases endpoints.

sections.title: Create databases
sections.body: POST to `/v1/databases` with `parent` (page_id), `title`, and `properties` schema. Each property key is the column name and the value defines the type and options. Supported types: title, rich_text, number, select, multi_select, date, checkbox, url, email, phone_number, relation, rollup, formula, people, files, created_time, last_edited_time. The response includes the new `database_id`. See [[notion/pages#create]] for adding rows to the database.

sections.title: Query and filter
sections.body: POST to `/v1/databases/{id}/query` with optional `filter`, `sorts`, `start_cursor`, and `page_size`. Filters are compound: use `and`/`or` arrays to combine conditions, or `property`-level conditions for single filters. Each condition specifies the property name, type, and operator (equals, contains, greater_than, before, etc.). Filters cannot reference formula or rollup intermediate values directly — filter on the result type instead. See [[notion/pages#properties]] for property value formats.

sections.title: Sorting results
sections.body: Sorts are an ordered array applied sequentially. Each sort object has `property` (column name) and `direction` (`ascending` or `descending`). Alternatively, sort by `timestamp` with value `created_time` or `last_edited_time`. Place the primary sort first. Sorting is server-side and applied before pagination.

sections.title: Property types
sections.body: Title (required, one per database), rich_text, number (with format: number, percent, dollar, etc.), select (single option), multi_select (multiple options), date (start and optional end), checkbox, url, email, phone_number, relation (links to another database), rollup (aggregate over relation), formula (computed from other properties), people (workspace members), files (uploaded or external), created_time, created_by, last_edited_time, last_edited_by. See [[notion/pages#properties]] for setting values on pages.

sections.title: Pagination
sections.body: Responses include `results` (array of pages), `has_more` (boolean), and `next_cursor` (string or null). Pass `next_cursor` as `start_cursor` in subsequent queries. Maximum `page_size` is 100, default is 100. Loop until `has_more` is false. Be mindful of [[notion#rate-limits]] when paginating large datasets — add delays between requests if needed.

scripts.name: Query Database
scripts.description: Query a Notion database with filters, sorts, and pagination

filter.description: Compound filter object with and/or/property conditions

sorts.description: Array of sort objects with property and direction

results.description: Array of page objects matching the query

nextCursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page of results

depends.reason: A Notion integration token is required and the database must be shared with the integration
